tasks.title: Rebuild the certification system.
tasks.description: Rebuild the certification system based upon version two courses that don't necessarily have a foundation but a dynamic pathway for the students based upon an assessment.

tasks.title: Ingest the recent edu board meeting content
tasks.description: Ingest the content and give AI the opportunity to make recommendations for future features and direction.

tasks.title: Consolidate Firebase client config into shared module
tasks.description:
firebase-helpers.js (introduced in Task #6) hardcodes the Firebase client config block (apiKey, projectId, appId, etc.), duplicating what is already present in students.html, transcript.html, and dashboard.html. Firebase client config is not a secret, but duplication creates a maintenance sync burden — a project change requires updating all copies.

Extract the config into a shared module (e.g. ai-academy/js/firebase-config.js) and import it from all consumers. Verify all pages still initialize Firebase correctly after the change.

tasks.title: Build the new cert program for v2
Convert courses to in person courses
tasks.description: Create a capability to convert individual classes to in person classes.

tasks.title: Student Assessment Evaluation
tasks.description: The student assessment tool is built. Evaluate the existing implementation against the May 19 EDU Board meeting transcript to ensure all discussed requirements were applied — interests + experience + knowledge inputs, employment skill mapping outputs, recommended course pathway, and any persona/feedback calibration discussed. Identify and log any gaps.

tasks.title: Student Portfolio System
tasks.description: Students accumulate artifacts from v2 artifact-development labs (web pages, apps, policies, etc.) into a shareable portfolio. Three of every eight lessons in v2 are artifact-building lessons — this system captures those outputs. Portfolio becomes interview and employer evidence. Prerequisite to the Real-World Partner Program (#17).

tasks.title: Real-World Partner Program (Nonprofit Track)
tasks.description: Structured program pairing nonprofits and community organizations with students in time-boxed project courses. The partner org provides a real problem; students build a working solution as their course artifact; the outcome goes on their portfolio. Model: CV Tech + Habitat for Humanity. Establishes market credibility and a B2B channel. Scott identified this as a potential mission shift after the May 19 EDU Board meeting. First step: identify 2-3 pilot nonprofit partners, define offer structure, and design the course format.

tasks.title: Lab AI Critical Feedback Mode
tasks.description: Add a lab configuration option to set an AI persona that gives honest, critical assessment rather than the default sycophantic praise. The persona should push back, identify what won't work, and not celebrate effort over outcomes. Susan Levaque raised this as a gap in current AI feedback loops during the May 19 EDU Board meeting — she noted AI needs to be explicitly told 'stop telling me how wonderful this is, tell me what's not going to work.'

tasks.title: AI Governance / NIST AI RMF Course
tasks.description: Build a v2 course specifically mapped to the NIST AI Risk Management Framework. Tejas Shroff identified a gap between existing AI content and AI governance/risk management expectations in enterprise and academic contexts. Scott has an existing AI governance tool with NIST AI RMF and HIPAA mapping already built — use that as primary source material. Course should address governance, risk identification, and compliance mapping.

tasks.title: Scouting America Merit Badge Content Partnership
tasks.description: Explore creating content aligned to Scouting America's new AI and cybersecurity merit badges. Tejas Shroff is a registered SME for Scouting America in this area and offered to explore getting Aesop into official merit badge materials. Reach: large youth audience, highly motivated learners, and a resume-building frame already built into merit badge culture. First step: Tejas to make an introduction; determine what content gaps exist in current merit badge materials.
